## Terms of reference endorsed for Williams Lake indoor sports facility Published 3:09 pm Tuesday, July 21, 2026 **By rebeccadyok** (File photo) A request for proposals for an indoor sport facility feasibility study in Williams Lake has gone out and submissions are expected before the end of next month. Williams Lake city council unanimously endorsed the terms of reference for the Indoor Sport Facility Working Group at a Tuesday, July 14 regular council meeting, with the Cariboo Regional District Board also endorsing the terms of reference at a regular board meeting on Friday, July 10. items.title: 🔓 [Williams Lake Tribune] Emergency department unavailable 10 hours in Williams Lake
items.content_html:
🎨 🌾 🌲 🏔️ 🔭 ✏️

## Emergency department unavailable 10 hours in Williams Lake Published 8:19 pm Tuesday, July 21, 2026 **By Williams Lake Tribune Staff** Cariboo Memorial Hospital in Williams Lake. (File photo) Interior Health (IH) is advising Williams Lake and area residents of temporary changes to emergency department hours at Cariboo Memorial Hospital. According to a news release, emergency services will be unavailable from 8 p.m. Tuesday, July 21, to 6 a.m. Wednesday, July 22. “Patients can access care at 100 Mile District General Hospital in 100 Mile House during this time,” IH stated in the release.

Premier David Eby at a town hall at the Simon Fraser University's Surrey campus on March 14, 2025\. The charges were approved by the BC Prosecution Service, and on Monday, July 20, Jonathan Crant was arrested. ## Effort to recall B.C. MLA Dallas Brodie falls short Published 4:09 pm Monday, July 20, 2026 One B.C. MLA Dallas Brodie speaks to media in the legislature on Thursday, Oct. 23, 2025\. (Mark Page/Black Press Media) The petition to recall Vancouver-Quilchena MLA Dallas Brodie, leader of the OneBC Party, has failed to gather the necessary number of signatures by a wide margin. For a petition to be successful, registered canvassers must collect signatures from 40 per cent or more of the 15,232 people who were eligible to vote in the Vancouver-Quilchena riding on Oct. 19, 2024. Petitioners

Liberal cabinet minister Mike Bernier to lead CentreBC party Published 3:26 pm Monday, July 20, 2026 Mike Bernier introduced himself as the new leader of CentreBC in Victoria on Saturday, July 18, 2026\. (Photo courtesy John Yanyshyn/CentreBC) Former B.C. Liberal cabinet minister Mike Bernier is the new leader of the CentreBC party. Bernier was a member of the B.C. legislature for 11 years, representing the Liberal party through its rebranding to B.C. United in 2023\. He is also the former mayor of Dawson Creek and spent 20 years working in the oil and gas industry. Founding

## BCWS responding to 13 out of control wildfires throughout Cariboo Fire Centre Published 12:38 pm Sunday, July 19, 2026 **By Patrick Davies** 1/2 The French Bar Creek wildfire on Saturday, July 18\. (BC Wildfire Service) The BC Wildfire Service continues to respond to 32 active wildfires in the Cariboo Fire Centre on Sunday, July 19, 13 of which are out of control. Most of the out-of-control wildfires were discovered in the last two days, following thundershowers which rolled through the Cariboo Fire Centre on Friday, July 17\. ## Decades-long journey brings Anita de Koning to Ashcroft Published 10:00 am Saturday, July 18, 2026 **By Cliff Marsh** Anita de Koning has been active with the Ashcroft-Cache Creek Seniors' Centre for many years. (Barbara Roden/Ashcroft-Cache Creek Journal) Anita de Koning made an epic journey to get to Ashcroft. The Hague, South Holland is where she was born into the turmoil of World War II, but she was fortunate to be too young to fully understand the atrocities of that time. In 1953, drawn to the allure of Canada and the seemingly limitless opportunities to nurture his family, her father

## Severe thunderstorm warning in effect for across Cariboo Published 12:02 pm Friday, July 17, 2026 **By Laisa Conde** Severe thunderstorm warning issued for Cariboo region. (Environment Canada photo) Environment Canada has issued a severe thunderstorm watch for the Cariboo region. The watch was issued by the weather department at 11:16 a.m. on Friday, July 17, for north Cariboo, including Quesnel, south Cariboo, including Williams Lake, 100 Mile House and the Chilcotin region. “Conditions are favourable for the development of severe thunderstorms that may be capable of producing strong wind
